title: Polycarpon lancifolium
taxonomy: Polycarpon lancifolium is a species of flowering plant in the family Caryophyllaceae. It belongs to the genus Polycarpon and is commonly known as small teasel or slender spurge-lover. This plant is native to Europe and parts of Asia. Polycarpon lancifolium is classified as a perennial herb, typically growing in moist environments. Its classification as a species is recognized within the Angiosperm Phylogeny Group classification system.

intro: Polycarpon lancifolium, also known as prickly mouse-eared, is a small, perennial herbaceous plant native to the Mediterranean region. The plant has been traditionally used for its medicinal properties, with reported health benefits including anti-inflammatory and antioxidant effects, as well as its potential to aid in the treatment of various digestive issues. Bioactive constituents of Polycarpon lancifolium include flavonoids, phenolic acids, and terpenoids, which are believed to be responsible for its therapeutic properties. The plant is often used in traditional herbal medicine and is typically prepared in the form of teas, infusions, or tinctures, although modern pharmaceutical applications are also being explored.
